channel.title: Tanner Waldo
channel.description: Writing on the structure, culture, and leadership that make work worth doing.

item.title: Ten Canadian Legal Cases Every New Manager Needs to Know
item.description: A tour of the decisions that quietly shape your handbook, from constructive dismissal to the duty to accommodate.

item.title: The PIP Problem: Are We Actually Trying to Help?
item.description: Why most performance improvement plans fail before the first check-in, and what an honest one looks like.

item.title: Good Intentions Don't Erase Discrimination
item.description: A small-business human rights case and the limits of the "we were trying to help" defence.

item.title: The Myth of the Unpaid Tryout
item.description: A 2025 Employment Standards appeal on trial shifts and what counts as paid work.

item.title: Unlocking High Performance — Jason Lauritsen
item.description: A frontline-HR read of Lauritsen's argument that performance is a relationship, not a form.

item.title: What Trust Is Made Of
item.description: Three things have to be working at once for a workplace to produce trust, and the instinct to fix the wrong one is what costs companies the most.
